PawnDiary.DiaryHumorCueDef.label: understatement-coda
PawnDiary.DiaryHumorCueDef.rule: End the entry with one flat understatement that shrinks what happened. No exaggeration, no punchline, no reaction.

PawnDiary.DiaryHumorCueDef.label: absurd-detail
PawnDiary.DiaryHumorCueDef.rule: Include exactly one small absurd-but-true detail. State it plainly, then continue without commenting on it.

PawnDiary.DiaryHumorCueDef.label: petty-complaint
PawnDiary.DiaryHumorCueDef.rule: Frame the event as a petty complaint about one object or the weather. Keep the facts accurate.

PawnDiary.DiaryHumorCueDef.label: misplaced-priority
PawnDiary.DiaryHumorCueDef.rule: Mention one small inconvenience as if it mattered more than the event itself. Do not explain the mismatch.

PawnDiary.DiaryHumorCueDef.label: deadpan-inventory
PawnDiary.DiaryHumorCueDef.rule: List what happened as a dry inventory of minor losses and gains. No emotion words.

PawnDiary.DiaryHumorCueDef.label: polite-regret
PawnDiary.DiaryHumorCueDef.rule: Close with one over-formal, slightly too-polite regret about something trivial.

PawnDiary.DiaryHumorCueDef.label: sideways-observation
PawnDiary.DiaryHumorCueDef.rule: Add one observation about an unrelated detail nearby, then stop. It should sit oddly beside the event.

PawnDiary.DiaryHumorCueDef.label: conditional-cop-out
PawnDiary.DiaryHumorCueDef.rule: End on a flat conditional like it could go either way, with no real concern.

PawnDiary.DiaryHumorCueDef.label: renamed-thing
PawnDiary.DiaryHumorCueDef.rule: Call one ordinary object by a slightly wrong dignified name, once, without explaining.

PawnDiary.DiaryHumorCueDef.label: shrug-closer
PawnDiary.DiaryHumorCueDef.rule: Close with one short line that treats the whole event as barely worth noting.

PawnDiary.DiaryHumorCueDef.label: routine-terrible
PawnDiary.DiaryHumorCueDef.rule: State the worst part as if it were routine inventory. No drama, no reflection, no pity.

PawnDiary.DiaryHumorCueDef.label: household-maintenance
PawnDiary.DiaryHumorCueDef.rule: End with one quiet line that treats the terrible thing as ordinary household maintenance.

PawnDiary.DiaryHumorCueDef.label: unacknowledged-dark
PawnDiary.DiaryHumorCueDef.rule: Let one detail be darker than the narrator acknowledges. They do not react to it at all.

PawnDiary.DiaryHumorCueDef.label: dry-tally-of-loss
PawnDiary.DiaryHumorCueDef.rule: Tally who or what was lost in a flat, clerical tone, like checking off chores.

PawnDiary.DiaryHumorCueDef.label: grim-understatement
PawnDiary.DiaryHumorCueDef.rule: Reduce the scale of the disaster with one calm understatement. Sincerity, not sarcasm.

PawnDiary.DiaryHumorCueDef.label: matter-of-fact-mortality
PawnDiary.DiaryHumorCueDef.rule: Mention death or injury the way one mentions the weather. Move straight on.

PawnDiary.DiaryHumorCueDef.label: queued-for-later
PawnDiary.DiaryHumorCueDef.rule: Treat the terrible event as an item deferred to a more convenient day.

PawnDiary.DiaryHumorCueDef.label: professional-detachment
PawnDiary.DiaryHumorCueDef.rule: Describe the aftermath with the detached tone of a routine work report.

PawnDiary.DiaryHumorCueDef.label: grim-practicality
PawnDiary.DiaryHumorCueDef.rule: Pivot immediately to one practical next task, as if the event were already settled.

PawnDiary.DiaryHumorCueDef.label: even-measure
PawnDiary.DiaryHumorCueDef.rule: Weigh the day's loss against some trivial gain and call it even, flatly.
